title: Correctness, Completeness, Optimality, Metrics, and Selection
description: A solver wins from one timing, path length is compared across different mazes, and explored-cell animation is mistaken for useful work. Learners cumulatively produce solver benchmark and decision report through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Validate endpoints, adjacency, passability, uniqueness policy, and reported cost against an independent route checker.
State the finite graph and resource assumptions under which a solver will find a path if one exists.
Tie shortest or least-cost claims to edge weights, heuristic properties, termination rule, and verified route cost.
